channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: I Played MindsEye's Blacklisted to Learn the Shocking Truth. It Made Me Take a Nap Instead.
item.content:encoded:
You know, honestly, I thought I was done with MindsEye. I gave the game a 2.5, had a lot of fun writing about it (and very little playing it), and just sort of walked away. Oh, sure, I’ve heard that they were going to make some DLC mission where director Leslie Benzies would reveal that he had proof people secretly sabotaged the game. I assumed it was just never going to happen. Then Blacklisted came out, and now I have to play it to see the nonsense.

Let me answer the questions you want answered first: Does Blacklisted provide a name of who sabotaged MindsEye? Of course not. At one point, the director of your assassin company (that I guess is a stand-in for Build a Rocket Boy) says there’s a “ritual network” that is trying to take them down. In a leaked rant from February, Ritual Network was the name of a YouTuber talent agency that Benzies claims was paying people to take down MindsEye. This is about the closest the game gets to naming anyone.

Outside of that, there’s one character, DJ Bunnyhause, that I think is supposed to be a stand-in for a YouTuber named Cyberboi, but that’s really it. So no, this is not the grand proof that was claimed, nor is it the hilarious crashout that we hoped for. If you are here hoping to see a disaster, sorry, you’ll just have to continue watching the actual goings on of Build a Rocket Boy.

Okay, so when Blacklisted isn’t being the equivalent of a poorly done subtweet, what is it about? The general story is that you play as Julia Black, an assassin who looks exactly like Lara Croft’s Doppleganger in Tomb Raider: Underworld. She used to work for a company of hitmen named Meridian, quit when her mom died, then got pulled back in by them to do some contract work, specifically to assassinate two people: Rohan and Carmen, who are drug dealers that are going to become weapons dealers, I think?

Part of the issue is that this is all entirely explained by a cutscene that I’m about 90% certain is using an AI voiceover. I don’t want to say 100% just in case, but the voice constantly pauses to think about words in a way that no normal human conversation flows, and at one point clearly reads a grammatical mistake out loud, so if it’s not AI, they sure got someone that talks exactly like one.

The other issue is that Blacklisted was clearly supposed to be the Hitman crossover level that MindsEye was going to have back when IO Interactive published it. Now that IOI isn’t the publisher anymore, that’s canceled, and it seems that this was quickly substituted in to take its place. It's very clear that Julia was supposed to be Agent 47, Meridian was supposed to be the ICA, and this was supposed to be a Hitman-styled level.

In the process of killing these two targets, Julia does stuff that, again, happens in a Hitman game, but Mindseye isn’t made for it, so it looks bad. At one point, you need to lure a cop away, kill him, then steal his outfit. Normal for Hitman, but MindsEye has to hold your hand to do this because you can only do this in the way MindsEye has decided you can. It’s one big tutorial for mechanics that never exist.

Also, Julia kills a cop, impersonates him to find and kill a YouTuber/DJ/streamer, then puts on his mask to break into a party. No one at any point questions why the famous DJ man is suddenly a woman wearing a Laura Croft-esque outfit that hides little. (Shortly after, Julia kills a Hispanic woman and impersonates her. No one questions why the Hispanic woman is suddenly white.) This also lets Julia go on a rant about how YouTubers just want to destroy and ruin things and are evil and mean and also—wait, I think that’s just Leslie Benzies again.

I think the DLC is about 30-45 minutes long, but halfway through it, I decided to just take a nap instead. I strongly suggest that.

This is the point where a lawyer should have stepped in and said “you need to stop,” but I have a feeling they all decided to jump ship. Somewhere between Benzies being mentioned in the Epstein files, Build a Rocket Boy laying off developers, and the remaining employees suing due to the company’s allegedly insane surveillance on them, I have a feeling they saw the writing on the wall. Get out before you get trapped in this nonsense.

Finally, I’m not going to make a weird expansion to vaguepost what I want to say. I will just say it: It’s time for Leslie Benzies to leave the industry. He’s washed up, he’s done, he has nothing left in him. He should be blacklisted from the industry.

Hey. Hey, did you get it? Did you get what I did there?

I'm Sam. I have been playing video games since my parents brought home a PlayStation whenever that came out. Started writing for TechRaptor for 2016 and,… More about Samuel



https://techraptor.net/gaming/features/i-played-blacklisted-to-learn-shocking-truth-it-made-me-take-nap-instead
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
